options.a: is usually harmless because oxygen is more easily attached to haemoglobin than carbon monoxide, to a magnitude of 200 times.
options.b: can be cured by breathing into a plastic bag to retain the carbon monoxide.
options.c: is a complication when hyperventilating and requires its own special and individual treatment.
options.d: presents an extremely dangerous situation as the blood may not be able carry sufficient amounts of oxygen to vital cells and tissues of the body.
